cases.title: Warm cache serves first-page snapshot instantly on cut-list open after restart
preconditions: Roubo previously loaded the cut list for project P with the Jira plugin at instance I, producing a persisted cache file under ~/.roubo/
The application has been fully quit and relaunched
The external Jira instance is reachable but no in-process cache exists

steps.instruction: Open the cut list panel for project P

observations.expected: The cut list renders populated issue rows without waiting for a network response
The cache-state badge reads 'warm'
The last-updated indicator shows a relative time reflecting when the snapshot was written, not 'loading'

The first visible row renders from the persisted on-disk snapshot without waiting on a live network round-trip (rows paint while/before the live RPC resolves). The literal <200ms p95 first-paint budget for CLI-NFR-002 is owned and measured by CLI-TC-011; this e2e-level observation is its no-network-wait proxy.

steps.instruction: Wait up to 5 seconds for background revalidation to complete

observations.expected: The cache-state badge transitions to 'revalidating' while the background fetch is in flight
On completion the badge returns to 'warm' and the last-updated time resets to 'just now'
The issue list is swapped with fresh data without a skeleton/loading flash

steps.instruction: Verify the on-disk cache file exists with correct permissions

observations.expected: File mode is 0600 (owner read/write only, no group or world bits)
File content is parseable JSON containing no credential or token fields

cases.title: Cold load when no cache file exists shows skeleton then live data
preconditions: No cache file exists for project P and the current plugin/instance combination
The external issue source is reachable

steps.instruction: Open the cut list panel for project P

observations.expected: A skeleton/shimmer loading state is displayed immediately (no stale or warm content)
No cache-state badge is shown while loading

steps.instruction: Wait for the live network response to return

observations.expected: Issue rows replace the skeleton
A cache file is written to ~/.roubo/ with mode 0600 containing the first-page snapshot
Total round-trip time is no worse than today's baseline plus the cache write (write adds under 50ms per CLI-NFR-003)

cases.title: Cache key change on sort-field change yields a miss, not a stale hit
preconditions: A warm cache entry exists for project P with sort field 'key', direction 'asc', page size 50, Jira plugin v2.1.0, instance I

steps.instruction: Open the cut list and confirm the warm badge and cached rows appear

observations.expected: Cache-state badge shows 'warm'

steps.instruction: Change the sort field from 'Key' to 'Priority' using the sort picker

observations.expected: The list resets to page 1 and a fresh fetch is triggered, not the previously cached order

steps.instruction: Wait for the new result to load and verify a new cache entry is written

observations.expected: A separate cache entry is persisted keyed to sort=priority,dir=asc
The original sort=key cache entry is not overwritten

cases.title: Cache key change on plugin version bump yields a miss and evicts the old entry
preconditions: A warm cache entry exists for plugin jira-self-hosted at version 2.1.0 for project P
The plugin is updated to version 2.2.0

steps.instruction: Open (or reload) the cut list after the plugin version change

observations.expected: No 'warm' badge is shown; the list performs a cold network fetch
The stale v2.1.0 cache entry is not served to the user

steps.instruction: Confirm the old cache file is evicted/ignored and a new entry is written for v2.2.0

observations.expected: Cache eviction/miss for the old version is logged (per CLI-NFR-009) with no secret material
A new cache entry keyed to v2.2.0 is written after the live fetch

cases.title: Cache key change on integration instance (endpoint) change yields a miss; no cross-tenant serve
preconditions: A warm cache entry exists for integration instance https://jira-a.example.com for project P
The project's integration is reconfigured to https://jira-b.example.com

steps.instruction: Open the cut list after the integration reconfiguration

observations.expected: The list performs a cold fetch against the new instance; no warm badge is shown
Data from jira-a.example.com is never surfaced as a warm hit for jira-b.example.com

steps.instruction: Verify the old instance cache entry is evicted on integration reconfiguration

observations.expected: The eviction is observable in logs, attributed to integration reconfiguration
No credential or token material from either instance appears in the cache file or logs

cases.title: Plugin uninstall evicts all associated cache entries
preconditions: Warm cache entries exist for two projects both using plugin jira-self-hosted

steps.instruction: Uninstall the jira-self-hosted plugin via the Roubo settings UI

observations.expected: All cache files keyed to jira-self-hosted are deleted or invalidated

steps.instruction: Open the cut list for one of the affected projects

observations.expected: A cold load (skeleton) is shown; no warm badge
No cache hit is served from the removed plugin's entries

cases.title: Project unregister evicts all associated cache entries
preconditions: A warm cache entry exists for project P

steps.instruction: Unregister project P via the Roubo projects settings

observations.expected: Cache files scoped to project P are deleted

steps.instruction: Re-register project P and open the cut list

observations.expected: A cold load is performed; no stale data from the previous registration is served

cases.title: Corrupt cache file is treated as a cold miss and never causes a fatal error
preconditions: A cache file exists for project P but has been corrupted (truncated JSON, invalid UTF-8, or zeroed bytes)

steps.instruction: Start the Roubo application with the corrupted cache file present

observations.expected: The application starts without a fatal or startup error

steps.instruction: Open the cut list for project P

observations.expected: A cold load (skeleton) is shown; no 'warm' badge
No error dialog or crash is surfaced to the user
A corrupt-file-discard log event is emitted (per CLI-NFR-009) with no secret material

steps.instruction: Wait for the live fetch to complete

observations.expected: A fresh cache file is written replacing the corrupt entry
The list renders normally from the live data

cases.title: Plugin down: last snapshot served with a visible stale indicator
preconditions: A warm cache entry exists for project P
The external integration is unreachable (network fault injected)

steps.instruction: Open the cut list for project P

observations.expected: The cached snapshot rows are displayed immediately
The cache-state badge shows 'stale'
A stale-snapshot banner appears indicating the plugin is unavailable and showing the snapshot age

steps.instruction: Restore the integration and click Retry in the stale banner

observations.expected: A background revalidation begins (badge transitions to 'revalidating')
On success the badge returns to 'warm' and fresh data is shown

cases.title: Cache file is written with mode 0600 and contains no credential material
preconditions: The integration is configured with an OAuth token stored in the OS keyring
The cut list has been loaded and a cache file written for project P

steps.instruction: Inspect the cache file permissions under ~/.roubo/ using stat or equivalent

observations.expected: File mode is exactly 0600: owner read+write only, no group or world permissions

steps.instruction: Search the cache file content for credential field names ('token', 'password', 'secret', 'apiKey', 'Authorization', 'access_token', 'refresh_token', 'client_secret')

observations.expected: None of the credential field names are present as keys or values in the cache file

steps.instruction: Verify a cache file created during revalidation also has mode 0600

observations.expected: The post-revalidation file also has mode 0600

cases.title: Warm load time-to-first-paint is under 200ms p95 on application restart
preconditions: A valid warm cache entry exists for project P
The application has been quit and relaunched (cold process, warm disk cache)

steps.instruction: Instrument cut-list panel mount with a high-resolution timer and open the cut list

observations.expected: Time from panel mount to first visible populated row is recorded

steps.instruction: Repeat the open-and-measure sequence 20 times across clean relaunches to capture p95

observations.expected: p95 first-meaningful-paint time is below 200ms

cases.title: Cache read+write overhead adds less than 50ms per request and does not regress cold load
preconditions: The issues route is instrumented to measure latency with and without cache I/O
A representative first-page payload (~50 issues) fixture is available

steps.instruction: Benchmark the issues route with caching disabled (baseline) for 50 iterations

observations.expected: Baseline p95 latency is recorded

steps.instruction: Enable caching and benchmark for 50 iterations including cache write (first call) and cache read (warm calls)

observations.expected: Cache-write overhead adds less than 50ms over baseline p95
Cache-read overhead adds less than 50ms over baseline p95
Cold first-load p95 is no worse than baseline beyond the cache write

cases.title: Cache lifecycle events are logged without secret material
preconditions: A mock logger is injected into the cache service
Fixtures cover: warm hit, cold miss, revalidation, corrupt-file discard, stale serve

steps.instruction: Trigger a warm hit, a cold miss, a background revalidation, a corrupt-file discard, and a stale serve in turn

observations.expected: The logger records a distinct event for each path (cache-hit, cache-miss, cache-revalidating, cache-corrupt-discard, cache-stale-serve)

steps.instruction: Inspect all logged payloads for credential field names

observations.expected: No log payload contains 'token', 'password', 'secret', 'apiKey', 'Authorization', 'access_token', 'refresh_token', or 'client_secret'

cases.title: Revalidation failure does not crash the app and retains the stale snapshot
preconditions: A warm cache entry exists for project P
The background revalidation fetch is configured to reject with a network error

steps.instruction: Open the cut list (warm snapshot served, revalidation begins in background)

observations.expected: Warm data is rendered immediately

steps.instruction: Allow the background revalidation to fail

observations.expected: The application does not crash or throw an unhandled rejection
The badge transitions to 'stale' and the stale banner appears
The previously served snapshot remains visible (not cleared)
A stale-serve log event is emitted per CLI-NFR-009

cases.title: Refresh button shows a spinning in-progress state when refresh is triggered
preconditions: The cut list panel is open showing a warm cached snapshot
The refresh control is visible and not spinning

steps.instruction: Click the refresh button in the cut list header

observations.expected: The refresh icon begins spinning
The last-updated indicator changes to 'refreshing...'
The cache-state badge changes to 'revalidating'

cases.title: In-progress state clears and last-updated text updates after a successful refresh
preconditions: A refresh has been initiated and the spinner is active

steps.instruction: Wait for the background revalidation request to complete successfully
Observe the refresh control and header indicators after the response arrives

observations.expected: The refresh icon stops spinning
The last-updated indicator shows a recent timestamp such as 'updated just now'
The cache-state badge returns to 'warm'
Fresh issue data is displayed

cases.title: E2E: open cut list, refresh, see spinner and refreshing text, data swaps in, indicator reads updated just now
preconditions: Roubo is running with a project that has a configured integration plugin
A warm first-page snapshot exists on disk

steps.instruction: Open the cut list panel for the project

observations.expected: The cached snapshot is displayed immediately (warm serve)
The last-updated indicator shows a time-ago string such as 'updated 2m ago'
The cache-state badge reads 'warm'

steps.instruction: Click the refresh button

observations.expected: The refresh icon begins spinning
The last-updated indicator changes to 'refreshing...'
Existing issue rows remain visible during revalidation (no blank flash)

steps.instruction: Wait for the revalidation response to return
Observe the cut list after revalidation completes

observations.expected: The spinner stops
The last-updated indicator reads 'updated just now'
The cache-state badge returns to 'warm'
The issue list reflects the latest data from the plugin

cases.title: Last-updated indicator shows a distinct stale-snapshot state when the plugin is unavailable
preconditions: The active integration plugin is unavailable or returns an error
A previous successful snapshot exists on disk

steps.instruction: Observe the cut list header indicators when the plugin is unavailable

observations.expected: The last-updated indicator uses a distinct 'snapshot 14m ago' format, not the normal 'updated N ago'
The cache-state badge reads 'stale' and is visually distinct from 'warm'

steps.instruction: Compare the stale indicator against the warm state

observations.expected: The stale state is unambiguously different in wording and colour from the warm updated state
A stale banner is shown identifying the plugin as unavailable and stating the snapshot age

cases.title: Clicking refresh while a refresh is already in progress does not start a second concurrent refresh
preconditions: A refresh is already in progress (spinner active)

steps.instruction: While the spinner is running, click the refresh button again

observations.expected: No second network request is issued to the plugin
The spinner continues without restarting or flickering
The last-updated indicator remains on 'refreshing...' without resetting

cases.title: Refresh state and completion are announced via an ARIA polite live region
preconditions: The cut list panel is open
A screen reader or ARIA live-region monitor is active

steps.instruction: Click the refresh button

observations.expected: A polite live region (aria-live='polite') announces 'Refreshing cut list' or equivalent

steps.instruction: Wait for the revalidation to complete

observations.expected: The polite live region announces 'Cut list updated' or equivalent on completion

steps.instruction: Verify the refresh button has an accessible label at all times

observations.expected: The refresh button carries aria-label='Refresh cut list' or equivalent, exposed to assistive technology regardless of refresh state

cases.title: Last-updated timestamp moves forward with each successful refresh
preconditions: The cut list panel is open in the warm state with a known last-updated timestamp

steps.instruction: Record the current last-updated indicator value
Click refresh and wait for the first revalidation to complete

observations.expected: The last-updated indicator updates to a more recent time than the recorded value

steps.instruction: Click refresh again and wait for the second revalidation to complete

observations.expected: The indicator updates again; each successful refresh moves the timestamp forward, not backward

cases.title: Next button advances to page 2 and the page indicator updates
preconditions: Cut list is loaded with warm cached data (page 1 of N, N > 1)
Prev button is disabled; Next button is enabled

steps.instruction: Click the Next button in the pagination footer

observations.expected: The list body renders the second page of items
The page indicator reads 'Page 2 of N'
Prev button becomes enabled

cases.title: Prev button is disabled on page 1
preconditions: Cut list is loaded with warm cached data showing page 1

steps.instruction: Observe the Prev button state on page 1

observations.expected: Prev button has the disabled attribute and is styled as inactive
The page indicator reads 'Page 1 of N'

steps.instruction: Attempt to activate the Prev button (click or Enter/Space)

observations.expected: No page change occurs and no error is thrown

cases.title: Prev navigation returns to the previous page using the retained cursor
preconditions: Cut list is loaded with warm cached data (at least 3 pages)

steps.instruction: Click Next twice to reach page 3
Click Prev to return to page 2

observations.expected: The list shows the same items that appeared on page 2 during forward navigation
The page indicator reads 'Page 2 of N'

steps.instruction: Click Prev again to return to page 1

observations.expected: The list shows the first-page items and the indicator reads 'Page 1 of N'
Prev button becomes disabled

cases.title: Next button is disabled on the last page
preconditions: Cut list is loaded with warm cached data with a known total page count N

steps.instruction: Click Next repeatedly until the indicator reads 'Page N of N'
Observe and attempt to click the Next button

observations.expected: Next button is disabled and styled inactive
No navigation occurs; the indicator still reads 'Page N of N'

cases.title: Changing the sort field resets navigation to page 1 and discards cursor history
preconditions: Cut list is loaded with warm cached data (at least 2 pages); user is on page 2

steps.instruction: Click Next to navigate to page 2
Change the sort field from the sort picker

observations.expected: The list resets to page 1 and the indicator reads 'Page 1 of N'
Prev button is disabled
Items are re-ordered according to the new sort field

cases.title: Pagination pager is hidden when the result set is empty
preconditions: Cut list is loaded with warm cached data

steps.instruction: Apply a filter combination that produces zero matching items

observations.expected: The empty state UI is displayed ('Nothing to pick up')
The pagination footer is not visible

cases.title: Prev/Next page step renders within 150ms p95 from loaded/cached state
preconditions: Cut list is loaded with warm cached data (page 1 of 3); all data is in memory

steps.instruction: Start a performance timer immediately before clicking Next, then click Next

observations.expected: Page 2 content is visible and the indicator reads 'Page 2 of 3'

steps.instruction: Stop the timer when page 2 finishes rendering; repeat 20 times for Next and Prev

observations.expected: Elapsed time is below 150ms at p95 for both Next and Prev steps

cases.title: Pagination controls are fully keyboard operable with visible focus
preconditions: Cut list is loaded with warm cached data (at least 2 pages); no mouse is used

steps.instruction: Tab to the Next button

observations.expected: Next button receives a visible amber-500 focus ring

steps.instruction: Press Enter or Space to activate Next

observations.expected: Navigation advances to page 2 and the indicator updates

steps.instruction: Tab to Prev and activate it with Enter or Space

observations.expected: Prev receives visible focus and navigation returns to page 1; on page 1 Prev becomes disabled

cases.title: Page change is announced to screen readers via the live region
preconditions: Cut list is loaded with at least 2 pages
A screen reader is active and the aria-live='polite' region is present

steps.instruction: Click the Next button

observations.expected: The screen reader announces the new page (e.g. 'Page 2') via the polite live region

steps.instruction: Click the Prev button

observations.expected: The screen reader announces 'Page 1' or equivalent

cases.title: Infinite scroll is absent: the list body does not grow on scroll
preconditions: Cut list is loaded with warm cached data (at least 2 pages)

steps.instruction: Scroll to the bottom of the list body on page 1

observations.expected: No additional items load automatically beyond the current page slice
The pagination footer (Prev/Next) is visible at the bottom

cases.title: E2E: page through the cut list, then change sort and be returned to page 1
preconditions: Cut list is loaded with warm cached data containing at least 3 pages of To Do items; user starts on page 1

steps.instruction: Observe the initial pagination footer

observations.expected: Page indicator reads 'Page 1 of N' (N >= 3)
Prev button is disabled; Next button is enabled

steps.instruction: Click Next to advance to page 2

observations.expected: Page indicator updates to 'Page 2 of N' and Prev becomes enabled
List body shows the second page of items, different from page 1

steps.instruction: Click Next again to advance to page 3, then click Prev to return to page 2

observations.expected: Page 3 shows its own items; returning to page 2 shows the same items seen during forward navigation (cursor retained)

steps.instruction: Change the sort field using the sort picker

observations.expected: Navigation resets to page 1 ('Page 1 of N')
Prev button is disabled and items are reordered by the new sort field

cases.title: Cut list defaults to ascending key order on first load with the Jira plugin
preconditions: Jira plugin is active and implements getSortFields returning key/updated/created/priority/rank
No saved sort preference exists for this project

steps.instruction: Open the cut list for a project with the Jira plugin active

observations.expected: The sort picker is rendered and shows 'Key' selected
The direction toggle shows ascending (aria-label 'Sort direction: ascending')
Issues are listed in ascending numeric key order (e.g. ROUBO-101 before ROUBO-104)

cases.title: Sort picker lists exactly the fields declared by the active plugin
preconditions: Jira plugin getSortFields returns key, updated, created, priority, rank

steps.instruction: Open the cut list and inspect the sort picker options

observations.expected: The picker contains exactly: Key, Recently updated, Created, Priority, Backlog rank

steps.instruction: Switch the active plugin to GitHub.com and inspect the sort picker options

observations.expected: The picker now contains exactly: Created, Recently updated, Comments
No 'Key', 'Priority', or 'Backlog rank' options are present

cases.title: E2E: open list ordered by key asc, switch to Recently updated desc, list reorders and resets to page 1, order stable across pages
preconditions: Jira plugin is active and returns multiple pages of issues
Cut list is loaded and on page 2 sorted by Key ascending

steps.instruction: Observe the current state: sort picker 'Key', ascending, on page 2

observations.expected: Sort picker selected value is 'Key' and direction is ascending
Page indicator reads 'Page 2 of N'

steps.instruction: Open the sort picker and select 'Recently updated'

observations.expected: The list resets to page 1 ('Page 1 of N')
Issues are ordered by last-updated
A live-region announcement is made (e.g. 'Sorted by Recently updated')

steps.instruction: Click the sort direction toggle to descending

observations.expected: The toggle aria-label becomes 'Sort direction: descending' and issues reorder to most recently updated first
The list resets to page 1

steps.instruction: Click Next to advance to page 2

observations.expected: Page 2 items continue in the same 'Recently updated descending' order
No item from page 1 reappears on page 2 (ordering stable, dedupe holds across the boundary)

cases.title: Plugin without getSortFields renders no sort picker and uses key-ascending order
preconditions: A plugin is active that does not implement getSortFields (returns MethodNotFound)

steps.instruction: Open the cut list with the non-implementing plugin active

observations.expected: No sort picker and no direction toggle are rendered
Issues are listed in the plugin's natural / key-ascending order
No error message, toast, or error state is surfaced

cases.title: Selecting a sort field triggers a source-side request with the correct sort parameters
preconditions: Jira plugin is active; outbound plugin requests can be observed

steps.instruction: Open the sort picker and select 'Priority'

observations.expected: The outbound list-issues request includes sort field 'priority' and direction 'asc'

steps.instruction: Toggle direction to descending

observations.expected: The outbound request includes sort field 'priority' and direction 'desc'
The request is issued to the source, not computed client-side from a cached full list

cases.title: MethodNotFound from getSortFields degrades to key-ascending default with no error
preconditions: Host-API is at version 1.2.0
Plugin stub returns MethodNotFound for the getSortFields RPC

steps.instruction: Invoke getSortFields against the stub via the host-API RPC layer

observations.expected: The host catches MethodNotFound without propagating it to the UI or throwing an unhandled exception

steps.instruction: Observe the cut list state after the RPC returns MethodNotFound

observations.expected: The sort picker is absent and issues display in key-ascending order
No error banner or toast is visible
A degradation event is logged (CLI-NFR-009) with no credential data

cases.title: Plugin built against host-API 1.0.0/1.1.0 keeps working via the MethodNotFound fallback with no incompatibility error
preconditions: Host-API is at version 1.2.0
Stub plugins declare host-API compatibility 1.0.0 and 1.1.0 and do not implement getSortFields

steps.instruction: Register the 1.0.0-compatible stub and open the cut list

observations.expected: The host reports no compatibility error or version-mismatch warning
No sort picker is shown; issues are in key-ascending order; pagination/filters/refresh still work

steps.instruction: Repeat with the 1.1.0-compatible stub that does not implement getSortFields

observations.expected: Identical behaviour: no picker, key-ascending order, no error

cases.title: Sort picker is fully keyboard operable and labelled
preconditions: Jira plugin is active and the sort picker is rendered; keyboard-only navigation

steps.instruction: Tab to the sort picker (the 'Sort by' control)

observations.expected: Focus reaches the picker with a visible amber-500 focus ring
The picker has an accessible label 'Sort by'

steps.instruction: Use the keyboard to change the selected sort field

observations.expected: The selection changes and the list reorders accordingly

steps.instruction: Tab to the direction toggle and press Enter or Space

observations.expected: Focus is visible; direction toggles and the aria-label updates to reflect the new state

cases.title: Ordering is stable across page boundaries: no duplication or gap between pages
preconditions: Jira plugin is active; the source has more than one page of issues

steps.instruction: Load the cut list sorted by Key ascending and note page 1 item keys

observations.expected: Page 1 items are in ascending key order with no duplicates

steps.instruction: Click Next to advance to page 2

observations.expected: Page 2 items continue in ascending key order from where page 1 ended
No page-1 key appears on page 2 and no gap exists between the pages

cases.title: Plugin declares a sort field it cannot truly apply: host renders the plugin's order without client re-sort
preconditions: A plugin stub declares 'rank' in getSortFields but returns a fixed order regardless of the requested sort

steps.instruction: Select 'Backlog rank' from the sort picker

observations.expected: The host sends the sort request with field 'rank'
The host renders whatever order the plugin returns without client-side re-sorting
No error is surfaced (the host trusts declared capabilities)

steps.instruction: Advance to page 2 and observe ordering continuity

observations.expected: Page 2 continues in the sequence the plugin returned, not re-ordered by the host

cases.title: GitHub plugin sort picker lists only declared fields (Created, Recently updated, Comments) with no Key option
preconditions: GitHub.com plugin is active and declares created, updated, comments

steps.instruction: Open the cut list with the GitHub.com plugin and inspect the sort picker

observations.expected: The picker contains exactly: Created, Recently updated, Comments
No 'Key' option is present (GitHub exposes no native item-key sort)
Default selected option is the first declared field ('Created')

cases.title: Sort selection and direction persist to per-project config and restore on reopen
preconditions: Jira plugin is active; the project has a writable roubo.yaml integration block

steps.instruction: Select 'Priority' and switch direction to descending

observations.expected: The picker shows 'Priority' and direction descending

steps.instruction: Close and reopen the cut list panel

observations.expected: The picker is pre-selected to 'Priority' descending and issues render in that order on first paint

steps.instruction: Inspect the project's roubo.yaml integration block (or per-user override)

observations.expected: The configured sort field and direction are recorded

cases.title: Cut list defaults to excluding In Progress and Done for Jira (only To Do shown)
preconditions: A project has the Jira self-hosted integration active with issues across To Do, In Progress, and Done
No per-project excludedStatusCategories override has been set

steps.instruction: Open the cut list for the project

observations.expected: Only To Do items are shown
The status summary reads 'Showing To Do only' with a hidden-by-status count

steps.instruction: Inspect the outgoing plugin query

observations.expected: The query excludes both 'In Progress' and 'Done' (e.g. statusCategory not in ('In Progress','Done'))
No In Progress or Done items appear in the response

cases.title: E2E: see only To Do, re-include In Progress via config, verify list updates and persists
preconditions: Jira integration active; issues in To Do and In Progress exist
No per-project override has been set (default: exclude In Progress and Done)

steps.instruction: Open the cut list for the project

observations.expected: Only To Do items are shown with a 'Showing To Do only' summary

steps.instruction: Click 'Configure' next to the status summary

observations.expected: The Status filter dialog opens listing To Do, In Progress, and Done
In Progress and Done are checked (excluded); To Do is disabled and cannot be excluded

steps.instruction: Uncheck 'In Progress' and click Save

observations.expected: The dialog closes and the cut list now shows both To Do and In Progress items
The hidden-by-status count decreases to reflect only Done being excluded

steps.instruction: Close and reopen the cut list for the same project

observations.expected: In Progress items are still visible (the per-project config persisted)

cases.title: E2E: first launch after upgrade shows the migration banner; Adjust opens config; Dismiss hides it permanently
preconditions: The user upgraded from a version where only Done was excluded by default
The migration banner has not yet been dismissed
The project has in-progress issues now hidden by the new default

steps.instruction: Launch Roubo and open the cut list for a project

observations.expected: The migration banner is visible, explaining the cut list now shows only To Do by default and hides In Progress and Done
The banner has an 'Adjust status filter' link and a 'Dismiss' button and role='status'

steps.instruction: Click 'Adjust status filter'

observations.expected: The Status filter dialog opens showing the current exclusion set (In Progress and Done checked)

steps.instruction: Cancel the dialog, then click 'Dismiss' on the banner

observations.expected: The banner disappears

steps.instruction: Close and reopen the cut list (or restart Roubo)

observations.expected: The migration banner does not reappear

cases.title: GitHub integration defaults to Closed-only exclusion when no faithful In Progress mapping exists
preconditions: A project has the GitHub.com integration active with open issues (some with an in-progress label) and closed issues
No per-project override has been set

steps.instruction: Open the cut list for the project

observations.expected: Closed issues are excluded; open issues (including in-progress-labelled) are shown by default

steps.instruction: Open the Status filter config dialog

observations.expected: The available categories are 'Open' and 'Closed' (not To Do/In Progress/Done)
Only 'Closed' is checked as excluded; 'Open' is disabled
A plugin note explains the approximation and that no faithful In Progress mapping exists

cases.title: Per-project status exclusion config persists across sessions and is scoped to the project
preconditions: Two projects A and B both use Jira; neither has a custom override

steps.instruction: For Project A, Configure, uncheck 'In Progress', and Save

observations.expected: Project A now shows To Do and In Progress items

steps.instruction: Open the cut list for Project B

observations.expected: Project B still shows only To Do items (default exclusion unchanged)

steps.instruction: Restart Roubo and open the cut list for Project A again

observations.expected: Project A still shows To Do and In Progress items (per-project config survived restart)

cases.title: Migration banner appears only once and does not reappear after dismiss or restart
preconditions: The user upgraded from the prior Done-only default; the banner has not been dismissed

steps.instruction: Open the cut list; the banner is visible. Click 'Dismiss'

observations.expected: The banner disappears

steps.instruction: Reload the panel and then restart Roubo and reopen the cut list

observations.expected: The banner does not reappear after reload or after restart (dismissal is persisted)

cases.title: Status config dialog: the To Do category cannot be excluded
preconditions: Jira integration active; the status config dialog is open

steps.instruction: Observe and attempt to check the 'To Do' row

observations.expected: The 'To Do' checkbox is disabled and cannot be checked
A note indicates the actionable category cannot be hidden

cases.title: Jira instance without statusCategory JQL support falls back to status-name exclusion
preconditions: A Jira self-hosted integration does not support the statusCategory JQL field
Issues exist across statuses representing To Do, In Progress, and Done

steps.instruction: Open the cut list for the project

observations.expected: The plugin falls back to a status-name exclusion list (e.g. excludes statuses named 'In Progress', 'In Review', 'Done', 'Closed')
Items matching the fallback in-progress/done status names are excluded
No error or warning is surfaced for the fallback path

steps.instruction: Open the status config dialog

observations.expected: The plugin note explains native categories are unavailable and status-name matching is used as a fallback

cases.title: Status config cancel does not change the exclusion set
preconditions: Jira integration active; default exclusion (In Progress and Done) is in effect

steps.instruction: Open the status config dialog and uncheck 'In Progress'
Click 'Cancel' instead of Save

observations.expected: The dialog closes without saving
The cut list still shows only To Do items (In Progress remains excluded)

cases.title: Only-To-Do with no To Do items shows the empty state gracefully
preconditions: Jira integration active; the project has zero To Do issues; In Progress and Done are excluded (default)

steps.instruction: Open the cut list for the project

observations.expected: An empty state is displayed ('No To Do items match the current filters')
The hidden-by-status count still reflects the non-To-Do items
No error is shown; the empty state is handled gracefully

cases.title: Status filter dialog is keyboard accessible and meets WCAG 2.1 AA
preconditions: Jira integration active; the cut list is open

steps.instruction: Tab to 'Configure' and activate it with Enter/Space

observations.expected: The Status filter dialog opens and focus moves into it (focus trap active)

steps.instruction: Tab through all controls in the dialog

observations.expected: All checkboxes, Cancel, and Save are reachable by keyboard with a visible amber-500 focus ring
The disabled 'To Do' checkbox announces its disabled state to assistive technology

steps.instruction: Press Escape and inspect dialog semantics

observations.expected: Escape closes the dialog without saving and focus returns to 'Configure'
The dialog has role='dialog', aria-modal='true', and an aria-labelledby pointing to its title

cases.title: Status exclusion is applied in-query (source-side), not as a client-side post-filter
preconditions: Jira integration active; outgoing plugin queries can be observed

steps.instruction: Open the cut list with default exclusions and inspect the query

observations.expected: The query sent to Jira includes statusCategory not in ('In Progress','Done') or an equivalent server-side filter
The server does not return In Progress or Done items (exclusion is not a client-side post-filter)

steps.instruction: Re-include In Progress via config, save, and inspect the next query

observations.expected: The updated query excludes only Done and In Progress items are returned

cases.title: Milestone facet menu lists only open milestones (Jira plugin)
preconditions: Jira self-hosted plugin active; the project has at least one active sprint and at least one closed sprint
The cut list panel is open

steps.instruction: Click the Milestone facet chip in the filter bar

observations.expected: The milestone dropdown opens
Only active/planned sprints are listed (e.g. 'Sprint 24 (active)', 'Sprint 25 (planned)')
No closed or archived sprints appear
A footer note reads 'Closed / archived milestones are excluded at the source.'

cases.title: Milestone facet menu lists only open milestones (GitHub.com plugin)
preconditions: GitHub.com plugin active; the project has at least one open and one closed milestone
The cut list panel is open

steps.instruction: Click the Milestone facet chip

observations.expected: Only open milestones are listed (e.g. 'v1.2 (open)', 'Backlog (open)')
Closed milestones are absent
The 'excluded at the source' footer note is visible

cases.title: E2E: open milestone filter (only live values), select a milestone, list filters and resets to page 1
preconditions: Jira self-hosted plugin active; issues exist for 'Sprint 24 (active)' and 'Sprint 25 (planned)'
The cut list is open showing To Do items

steps.instruction: Click the Milestone facet chip to open the menu

observations.expected: The dropdown lists only live milestones; no closed/archived milestone names appear

steps.instruction: Click 'Sprint 24 (active)'

observations.expected: The Milestone chip shows the active/selected state
The list refreshes to show only issues in 'Sprint 24 (active)'
Pagination resets to page 1 and a 'Filtered to milestone Sprint 24 (active)' announcement is made

steps.instruction: Click the Milestone chip again and select 'All milestones'

observations.expected: The filter clears and the full To Do list is shown; the chip returns to its default state

cases.title: GHE plugin implements the filterFacets and getFacetOptions host-API 1.1.0 contract (CLI-FR-016 prerequisite)
preconditions: The GitHub Enterprise plugin (post-FR-016) is installed and configured against a GHE instance
A project is registered with the GHE plugin active

steps.instruction: Open the cut list for the GHE-backed project

observations.expected: The cut list renders without error; no MethodNotFound or contract-mismatch error is surfaced

steps.instruction: Invoke filterFacets against the GHE plugin (contract test or direct RPC)

observations.expected: The GHE plugin responds with a valid filterFacets payload conforming to host-API 1.1.0, including milestone/label facets

steps.instruction: Invoke getFacetOptions for the 'milestone' facet against the GHE plugin

observations.expected: The response conforms to the host-API 1.1.0 getFacetOptions shape and returns only open milestones (closed excluded at source)

cases.title: Per-project tunables (sort field, direction, status exclusion) persist in roubo.yaml and survive restart
preconditions: Jira plugin active; the project's roubo.yaml has no existing cut-list integration block
The cut list is open

steps.instruction: Change the sort field to 'Priority' and switch direction to descending

observations.expected: The list reorders by priority descending

steps.instruction: Open the status filter dialog, uncheck 'In Progress', and Save

observations.expected: The cut list now shows To Do and In Progress items

steps.instruction: Inspect the project's roubo.yaml integration block

observations.expected: The block records the sort field 'priority', direction 'desc', and excludedStatusCategories without 'In Progress'

steps.instruction: Restart Roubo and reopen the same project's cut list

observations.expected: The sort picker shows 'Priority' (descending) and In Progress items appear, confirming persistence

cases.title: E2E: configure sort and status tunables per project and confirm they survive reload and are plugin-scoped
preconditions: Jira plugin active for Project A; the cut list is open; roubo.yaml has no cut-list integration block

steps.instruction: Select 'Backlog rank' from the sort picker and switch direction to descending

observations.expected: The list reorders by backlog rank descending and the direction aria-label reads 'Sort direction: descending'

steps.instruction: Click 'Configure', uncheck 'In Progress', and Save

observations.expected: An announcement 'Status filter saved' is made and the cut list now shows To Do and In Progress items

steps.instruction: Navigate away from the project and back to the cut list

observations.expected: The sort picker still shows 'Backlog rank' descending and In Progress items remain visible

steps.instruction: Switch to Project B (different active plugin) and open its cut list

observations.expected: Project B's sort picker shows only fields declared by its own plugin and its status config is independent of Project A's

cases.title: Sort and status config offer only the options the active plugin declares it supports
preconditions: Two projects: one using Jira, one using GitHub.com

steps.instruction: Open the Jira project's cut list and inspect the sort picker and status config

observations.expected: Sort picker lists Jira fields (Key, Recently updated, Created, Priority, Backlog rank); status config lists To Do/In Progress/Done

steps.instruction: Open the GitHub.com project's cut list and inspect the same controls

observations.expected: Sort picker lists Created/Recently updated/Comments (no Priority or Backlog rank); status config lists Open/Closed only

cases.title: applyFilters p95 runtime stays below 50ms for ~500 issues
preconditions: A dataset of ~500 mock issues is prepared (matching the existing cut-list-filter-recompute.perf fixture)
applyFilters is importable and the Vitest perf harness is available

steps.instruction: Run applyFilters 200 times against the 500-issue dataset with a milestone filter and a label filter active, measuring wall-clock per call

observations.expected: The p95 execution time is below 50ms
The function returns the correct filtered subset each run

steps.instruction: Repeat with no filters and with a zero-match filter

observations.expected: p95 remains below 50ms across the no-filter and zero-match scenarios

cases.title: Selecting a previously-active milestone that now has zero To Do items shows the empty state, not a hidden facet
preconditions: Jira plugin active; the 'Hardening' milestone is selected as the active filter; all its issues have moved to Done (excluded by default)

steps.instruction: Observe the cut list with 'Hardening' selected but its To Do issues exhausted

observations.expected: The empty state is shown ('No To Do items match the current filters'), not a crash or a removed chip
The Milestone chip still shows active so the user understands a filter is applied
The pagination footer is hidden

steps.instruction: Clear the milestone filter (select 'All milestones')

observations.expected: The empty state is replaced by the full To Do list

cases.title: Per-user override layered over roubo.yaml replaces arrays rather than merging them
preconditions: roubo.yaml has excludedStatusCategories ['In Progress','Done'] and sortField 'key'
The per-user override sets excludedStatusCategories ['Done'] and sortField 'priority'

steps.instruction: Open the cut list and inspect the active sort field

observations.expected: The sort picker shows 'Priority', confirming the override replaced (not merged) the roubo.yaml value

steps.instruction: Open the status config dialog and inspect the checked categories

observations.expected: Only 'Done' is excluded; 'In Progress' is unchecked (override array replaced the roubo.yaml array)
In Progress items appear in the cut list

cases.title: Closed Jira epics are excluded from the epic facet options
preconditions: Jira plugin active; the project has open epics and resolved/closed epics; the filter bar includes an Epic facet

steps.instruction: Open the Epic facet options in the filter bar

observations.expected: Only open/in-progress epics appear
Resolved or closed epics are absent
A note indicates closed/resolved epics are excluded at the source

cases.title: GHE plugin missing filterFacets (pre-FR-016) surfaces no facet options and no uncaught error
preconditions: The GHE plugin is at a version that has NOT implemented filterFacets/getFacetOptions (pre-FR-016 state)
A project is registered with this GHE plugin; the cut list is open

steps.instruction: Open the cut list and attempt to use the Milestone facet with the un-patched GHE plugin active

observations.expected: Either the Milestone chip is absent (capability not declared) or the dropdown opens with a graceful 'No options' message
No JavaScript error is thrown and the cut list remains functional
The host does not surface a contract-mismatch or MethodNotFound error to the user

cases.title: Cache-hit event fires on the majority of repeat cut-list loads in a session
preconditions: A mock logger is injected into the cache service
A cold seed load has populated the persistent cache for project P with unchanged sources/sort/filters

steps.instruction: After the cold seed, open the cut list 5 times in the session without changing sources, sort, filters, or status exclusion

observations.expected: The cache-hit log event fires on at least 4 of the 5 opens (the majority are served warm), exercising the 'cache hit rate' success indicator
At most one open records a cache-miss/revalidating event (the cold seed or a background revalidation), and no credential material appears in any logged event

cases.title: Saved sort field unsupported by the active plugin falls back to its first declared field with no error
preconditions: The project's roubo.yaml integration block records a sort field that the previously-active plugin declared (e.g. Jira's 'rank')

steps.instruction: Open a project whose saved sort field is 'rank' (declared only by jira-self-hosted), then switch its active integration to github-com, which declares created/updated/comments and has no 'rank'

observations.expected: The cut list loads and renders results without an error banner, toast, or surfaced error about the unsupported saved sort

steps.instruction: Inspect the sort picker after the plugin switch

observations.expected: The picker is selected to github-com's first declared field (Created), not the unsupported 'rank'
The list is ordered by that fallback field source-side, with no residual reference to 'rank'
